messages.content:
"Even if atmospheric composition were fixed today global-mean temperature and sea level rise would continue due to oceanic thermal inertia" (Wigley, 2005, p.1766). This is an example of:
A direct quote
An indirect quote
Paraphrasing
A direct quote

A claim to truth or a statement of fact which has not been supported with literature is called:


An assertion

A reference

An allegation
An assertion

Working with another student on the same assignment where both students submit similar work is known as:


Collusion

Plagiarism

Academic integrity
Collusion

Which of the following statements best describes the term plagiarism?


Copying directly or indirectly from a source without providing a full citation

Working with another student to produce similar output

Falsifying your data so that you get the results you want
Copying directly or indirectly from a source without providing a full citation

Which of the following best describes the analysis of the literature process?


Critiquing sources of literature

Combining similar sources of literature

Using literature to support your arguments
Combining similar sources of literature

Which of the following can be used as an approach to critiquing a source?


Using the reading list and sources recommended by your supervisor

Drawing together the sources to consider if there is a common theme throughout

Considering who, what, why, where, when and how
Considering who, what, why, where, when, and how

Which of the following is part of the process of your research being disseminated via a journal article?


Submitting an abstract to the editor-in-chief

Working with your supervisor to identify a suitable journal

Meeting with the editor-in-chief at a conference
Submitting an abstract to the editor-in-chief

An interview is a data collection method that collects data from:


One individual at a time

A group of people at the same time

Written responses from the research participant
One individual at a time

An advantage of using focus groups as a data collection method is that:


It enables a free-flowing discussion within the group

It is easy to get a similar group of people at the same time

Focus groups require little input from the researcher
It enables a free-flowing discussion within the group

Why might a researcher choose not to conduct focus groups as data collection method?


The participants may not be comfortable sharing their perspectives with others

You do not need informed consent to record a focus group

You do not need to consider the identity of the people who attend a focus group
The participants may not be comfortable sharing their perspectives with others.

A closed question on a questionnaire allows:


A respondent to provide a written comment

A respondent to record a single number, word or response

A respondent to expand on a previous question
A respondent to record a single number, word or response

A written report which focuses over a period of time on a person, group or situation is called a:


Case study

Diary

Form of secondary data
Case study

Which of the following statements about observational research is correct?


It employs all the senses to describe an event

People's views about an event are sought after the event has happened using a questionnaire

The data are collected from secondary sources
It employs all the senses to describe an event

A controlled experiment requires the researcher to:


Keep all conditions the same while altering one variable

Develop their own unique approach to testing the relationship between variables

Use a trial and error approach to test the relationship between random variables
Keep all conditions the same while altering one variable

A dependent variable is:


The condition that the researcher alters

The condition that the research measures

The condition which remains the same
The condition that the research measures

Which of the following is a characteristic of writer's block?


Being unable to direct your focus on meaningful output

Not knowing what needs to be completed

Being unable to collect your data
Being unable to direct your focus on meaningful output

Which of the following is part of the redrafting process?


Adding new material where required

Asking your supervisor to correct simple typographic errors

Checking work for accuracy, mistakes and mistyped words
Adding new material where required

Which of the following is an example of synthesis?


The 'greenhouse effect' is a factor that some researchers provide as a reason for global warming (Newell, 2000)

According to Fisher et al (2007), the 'greenhouse effect' is a contributory factor to global warming

The 'greenhouse effect' is a contributory factor to global warming (Fisher et al, 2007; Newell, 2000)
The 'greenhouse effect' is a contributory factor to global warming (Fisher et al, 2007; Newell, 2000)

Which of the following statements best describes the term collusion?


Not using references accurately within your work

Working with another student to produce similar output

Making a statement which is not supported with literature
Working with another student to produce similar output

Which of the following statements best describes the term plagiarism?


Copying directly or indirectly from a source without providing a full citation

Working with another student to produce similar output

Falsifying your data so that you get the results you want
Copying directly or indirectly from a source without providing a full citation

Which of the following best describes the analysis of the literature process?


Critiquing sources of literature

Combining similar sources of literature

Using literature to support your arguments
Using literature to support your arguments

Which of the following would be part of the process of analysing a source?


Considering if there are any core themes to the sources

Considering the relevance of the methodology used in the source to your proposed methodology

Considering whether the research within the source has since been updated through more recent research
Considering if there are any core themes to the sources

Which of the following is a useful strategy for disseminating your research?


Searching for relevant student conferences and submitting an abstract

Submitting your full dissertation to a conference so that they can tell you what they would like you to speak about

Searching for a conference with a speaker you would like to hear
Searching for relevant student conferences and submitting an abstract

A to-do list is useful for:


Planning well ahead

Identifying all the tasks you need to do

Identifying the non-academic tasks you need to do
Identifying all the tasks you need to do

Which of the following is an effective way to use your daily to-do list?


To timetable 100% of your time

To list all of your main commitments and tasks to deliver your project

To block out time to complete substantial tasks
To list all of your main commitments and tasks to deliver your project

Which of the following is part of the editing process?


Adding new material where necessary

Checking work for readability

Checking work for clarity
Checking work for clarity

Sharing your research to a wider audience is also known as:


Plagiarism

Dissemination

Collusion
Dissemination

Which of the following would be part of the process of analysing a source?


Considering if there are any core themes to the sources

Considering the relevance of the methodology used in the source to your proposed methodology

Considering whether the research within the source has since been updated through more recent research
All of the options mentioned would be part of the process of analyzing a source.
which one is correct
Considering if there are any core themes to the sources.
no
I apologize for the confusion. The correct option is: "Considering whether the research within the source has since been updated through more recent research."

Which of the following is a characteristic of writer's block?


Being unable to direct your focus on meaningful output

Not knowing what needs to be completed

Being unable to collect your data
Being unable to direct your focus on meaningful output

Which of the following best describes the review stage of the drafting process?


Carefully re-reading your first draft

Discussing the structure of the dissertation with your supervisor

Looking at the literature within your research topic
Carefully re-reading your first draft

Which of the following is part of the editing process?


Adding new material where necessary

Checking work for readability

Checking work for clarity
All of the options mentioned are part of the editing process.
which one is correct
Apologies for the confusion. The correct option is: "Checking work for readability."

Which of the following form part of the analysis of the literature process?


Deciding if two or more sources are at odds with any of the others

Developing meaning through combining different sources

Using only those sources that support your research question
Deciding if two or more sources are at odds with any of the others and developing meaning through combining different sources are both part of the analysis of the literature process.
which one is correct
Apologies for the confusion. The correct option is: "Deciding if two or more sources are at odds with any of the others."

Which of the following is a useful strategy for disseminating your research?


Searching for relevant student conferences and submitting an abstract

Submitting your full dissertation to a conference so that they can tell you what they would like you to speak about

Searching for a conference with a speaker you would like to hear
Searching for relevant student conferences and submitting an abstract.

Which of the following is an effective way to manage risk?


Including project slippage

Transference of some tasks

Using a Gantt chart
Using a Gantt chart.

If a stakeholder has high levels of interest and power:


Manage them closely yourself

Ask your supervisor to take responsibility for communicating with them

Avoid working too closely with them - they may cause problems
Manage them closely yourself.

The most important project stakeholder is:


Your supervisor

You

Your examiner
You
